units.name: Limits and Continuity
units.description: Students explore how limits allow us to understand function behavior near a point and at infinity, building the conceptual foundation for all of calculus.
key_skills: Estimate limits from graphs, tables & equations
Determine continuity at a point and on an interval

units.name: Differentiation: Definition and Fundamental Properties
units.description: Students apply limits to define the derivative and develop fluency with basic differentiation rules across multiple representations.
key_skills: Define and interpret the derivative as a rate of change
Apply power, product, and quotient rules
Determine differentiability and connect to continuity

units.name: Differentiation: Composite, Implicit, and Inverse Functions
units.description: Students master the chain rule, implicit differentiation, and derivatives of inverse functions, including higher-order derivatives.
key_skills: Apply the chain rule to composite functions
Find derivatives of inverse and inverse trig functions

units.name: Contextual Applications of Differentiation
units.description: Students apply derivatives to model and solve real-world problems involving rates of change, motion, and approximation.
key_skills: Set up and solve related rates problems

units.name: Analytical Applications of Differentiation
units.description: Students analyze relationships among a function and its derivatives to solve optimization problems and justify conclusions about behavior.
key_skills: Determine intervals of increase/decrease and critical points
Use first and second derivative tests
Determine concavity and locate inflection points

units.name: Integration and Accumulation of Change
units.description: Students apply limits to define definite integrals and discover how the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus connects differentiation and integration.
key_skills: Apply both parts of the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus

units.name: Differential Equations
units.description: Students solve differential equations and apply them to model exponential growth, decay, and other real-world phenomena.

units.name: Applications of Integration
units.description: Students use integration to solve problems involving net change, area between curves, and volumes of 3-D solids.
key_skills: Find the average value of a function
Find volume using disk and washer methods
Find volume using known cross-section shapes
